sourceMovie.title: The Girl Who Leapt Through Time
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Girl Who Leapt Through Time (2006) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Stand by Me Doraemon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Girl Who Leapt Through Time clicked for you, Stand by Me Doraemon (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Girl Who Leapt Through Time clicked for you, The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and animation, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Girl Who Leapt Through Time?
faq.answer: Try Stand by Me Doraemon, The Tunnel to Summer, the Exit of Goodbyes, Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl—each shares DNA with The Girl Who Leapt Through Time while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Girl Who Leapt Through Time?
faq.answer: The Girl Who Leapt Through Time (2006) is best described as Fantasy, Animation, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Girl Who Leapt Through Time worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
